title: cyphomandra divaricata
intro: Cyphomandra divaricata, also known as the tree tomato or tamarillo, is a perennial shrub native to South America and the Galapagos Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been found to help treat various health issues. The tree tomato has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the tree tomato is often used in teas, juices, and jams, and is also consumed raw or cooked in various dishes.
